generatedSentence: During a well-deserved leisure hiatus, Valkyrie, a kind driver, left her pajama-clad longhouse, a bastion of happiness, though she felt thirsty for purpose beyond her tiny kitty and kittens. Her juvenile manga collection offered light entertainment, balancing the dirty history of her past with a vision for improvement, all while tending to her loving efforts collecting geode ornaments as trophies. She discovered a mysterious oculus, which provided cryptic instructions, a direct call to action. A wrinkly lemur, a freaky figure from a heinous history of kings and knaves, appeared, having adjured the village to gobble eggplant and onions, imposing a strict vegetarian diet. He represented a looming threat, a 'blacker' entity seeking to drain the 'liver' of the land. His appearance coincided with a strange illness that made people visibly bleed, a kerfuffle of reality, forcing everyone to enter a new, dire forecast. The motor of society seemed to stop, jampacked with fear. The situation reached its climax when the drunken lemur, now revealed as the true antichrist, attempted to narrate a twisted vision for democracy, surrounded by knaves. He tried to sell a gallon of cheap wine and maple syrup from junky jukeboxes near the decadent docks, attempting to restock his illicit money supply. Valkyrie, now empowered, used a series of whips as anchors, employing tactical instructions from the oculus. She launched kayaks from the embankment, a pivotal milestone, causing a huge splash, her efforts an integral part of stopping the wrong, wack kleptomania. After the climatic struggle, where the lemur's power was broken, Valkyrie secured a turnkey victory, balancing the scales of justice. The barrier of his influence dissolved like an ornament from the floor. The therapy of her actions brought a level of happiness, a fulfillment not just for herself but for the flocks of vegetarians. She returned to her modern address, knowing the password for peace, her phones now quiet. This historical moment, an intermediate improvement, provided a new balance, a kilocalorie of hope for the world, a new number in the endless universals of life.
panelSentences: During a well-deserved leisure hiatus, Valkyrie, a kind driver, left her pajama-clad longhouse, a bastion of happiness, though she felt thirsty for purpose beyond her tiny kitty and kittens. Her juvenile manga collection offered light entertainment, balancing the dirty history of her past with a vision for improvement, all while tending to her loving efforts collecting geode ornaments as trophies. She discovered a mysterious oculus, which provided cryptic instructions, a direct call to action.

A wrinkly lemur, a freaky figure from a heinous history of kings and knaves, appeared, having adjured the village to gobble eggplant and onions, imposing a strict vegetarian diet. He represented a looming threat, a 'blacker' entity seeking to drain the 'liver' of the land. His appearance coincided with a strange illness that made people visibly bleed, a kerfuffle of reality, forcing everyone to enter a new, dire forecast. The motor of society seemed to stop, jampacked with fear.

The situation reached its climax when the drunken lemur, now revealed as the true antichrist, attempted to narrate a twisted vision for democracy, surrounded by knaves. He tried to sell a gallon of cheap wine and maple syrup from junky jukeboxes near the decadent docks, attempting to restock his illicit money supply. Valkyrie, now empowered, used a series of whips as anchors, employing tactical instructions from the oculus. She launched kayaks from the embankment, a pivotal milestone, causing a huge splash, her efforts an integral part of stopping the wrong, wack kleptomania.

After the climatic struggle, where the lemur's power was broken, Valkyrie secured a turnkey victory, balancing the scales of justice. The barrier of his influence dissolved like an ornament from the floor. The therapy of her actions brought a level of happiness, a fulfillment not just for herself but for the flocks of vegetarians. She returned to her modern address, knowing the password for peace, her phones now quiet. This historical moment, an intermediate improvement, provided a new balance, a kilocalorie of hope for the world, a new number in the endless universals of life.
rawPanelSentences: During a well-deserved leisure hiatus, Valkyrie, a kind driver, left her pajama-clad longhouse, a bastion of happiness, though she felt thirsty for purpose beyond her tiny kitty and kittens. Her juvenile manga collection offered light entertainment, balancing the dirty history of her past with a vision for improvement, all while tending to her loving efforts collecting geode ornaments as trophies. She discovered a mysterious oculus, which provided cryptic instructions, a direct call to action.

A wrinkly lemur, a freaky figure from a heinous history of kings and knaves, appeared, having adjured the village to gobble eggplant and onions, imposing a strict vegetarian diet. He represented a looming threat, a 'blacker' entity seeking to drain the 'liver' of the land. His appearance coincided with a strange illness that made people visibly bleed, a kerfuffle of reality, forcing everyone to enter a new, dire forecast. The motor of society seemed to stop, jampacked with fear.

The situation reached its climax when the drunken lemur, now revealed as the true antichrist, attempted to narrate a twisted vision for democracy, surrounded by knaves. He tried to sell a gallon of cheap wine and maple syrup from junky jukeboxes near the decadent docks, attempting to restock his illicit money supply. Valkyrie, now empowered, used a series of whips as anchors, employing tactical instructions from the oculus. She launched kayaks from the embankment, a pivotal milestone, causing a huge splash, her efforts an integral part of stopping the wrong, wack kleptomania.

After the climatic struggle, where the lemur's power was broken, Valkyrie secured a turnkey victory, balancing the scales of justice. The barrier of his influence dissolved like an ornament from the floor. The therapy of her actions brought a level of happiness, a fulfillment not just for herself but for the flocks of vegetarians. She returned to her modern address, knowing the password for peace, her phones now quiet. This historical moment, an intermediate improvement, provided a new balance, a kilocalorie of hope for the world, a new number in the endless universals of life.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A female Valkyrie character wearing a high-tech armored bodysuit with glowing blue accents and a winged helmet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Valkyrie (back to viewer) looking out a longhouse window at a futuristic cityscape, a tiny robotic kitty purring by her feet, soft morning light. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Close-up on a menacing, wrinkly lemur-like alien figure with glowing red eyes, gesturing emphatically towards a group of frightened villagers, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Valkyrie in dynamic action, wielding glowing energy whips, leaping from a futuristic kayak on a neon-lit embankment towards the lemur-like alien, dramatic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Valkyrie standing victorious amidst the now-peaceful cityscape, sunlight glinting off her armor, a sense of calm and resolution, wide shot.

definitions.definition: A pair of pajamas.
Clothes for wearing to bed and sleeping in, usually consisting of a loose-fitting shirt and pants/trousers.
Loose-fitting trousers worn by both sexes in various southern Asian countries including India.
One who drives something, in any sense of the verb to drive.
Something that drives something, in any sense of the verb to drive.
A person who drives a motorized vehicle such as a car or a bus.
A person who drives some other vehicle.
A program that acts as an interface between an application and hardware, written specifically for the device it controls.
A golf club used to drive the ball a great distance.
A kind of sail, smaller than a fore and aft spanker on a square-rigged ship, a driver is tied to the same spars.
A mallet.
A tamping iron.
A cooper's hammer for driving on barrel hoops.
A screwdriver.
To make (something) dirty.
To stain or tarnish (somebody) with dishonor.
To debase by distorting the real nature of (something).
To become soiled.
Unclean; covered with or containing unpleasant substances such as dirt or grime.
definitions.example: Despite a walk in the rain, my shoes weren't too dirty.

definitions.definition: That makes one unclean; corrupting, infecting.
definitions.example: Don't put that in your mouth, dear. It's dirty.

definitions.definition: Morally unclean; obscene or indecent, especially sexually.
definitions.example: At the reception, Uncle Nick got drunk and told dirty jokes to the bridesmaids.

definitions.definition: Dishonourable; violating accepted standards or rules.
definitions.example: He might have scored, but it was a dirty trick that won him the penalty.

definitions.definition: Corrupt, illegal, or improper.
definitions.example: I won't accept your dirty money!

definitions.definition: Out of tune.
definitions.example: You need to tune that guitar: the G string sounds dirty.

definitions.definition: Of color, discolored by impurities.
definitions.example: The old flag was a dirty white.

definitions.definition: Containing data needing to be written back to memory or disk.
definitions.example: Occasionally it reads the sector into a dirty buffer, which means it needs to sync the dirty buffer first.

definitions.definition: Carrying illegal drugs among one's possessions or inside of one's bloodstream.
definitions.example: None of y'all get into my car if you're dirty.

definitions.definition: Used as an intensifier, especially in conjunction with "great".
definitions.example: He lives in a dirty great mansion.

definitions.definition: Sleety; gusty; stormy.
definitions.example: dirty weather

definitions.definition: Of an alcoholic beverage, especially a cocktail or mixed drink: served with the juice of olives.
definitions.example: dirty martini

definitions.definition: Of food, indulgent in an unhealthy way.
definitions.example: The waiter served dirty burgers to the customers.

definitions.definition: In a dirty manner.
definitions.example: to play dirty

definitions.definition: To set (something, often a building) on fire.
To heat as with fire, but without setting on fire, as ceramic, metal objects, etc.
definitions.example: If you fire the pottery at too high a temperature, it may crack.

definitions.definition: To drive away by setting a fire.
To terminate the employment contract of (an employee), especially for cause (such as misconduct or poor performance).
To shoot (a gun or analogous device).
definitions.example: He fired his radar gun at passing cars.

definitions.definition: To shoot a gun, cannon, or similar weapon.
definitions.example: Don't fire until you see the whites of their eyes.

definitions.definition: To shoot; to attempt to score a goal.
To cause an action potential in a cell.
definitions.example: When a neuron fires, it transmits information.

definitions.definition: To forcibly direct (something).
definitions.example: He answered the questions the reporters fired at him.

definitions.definition: (computer sciences, software engineering) To initiate an event (by means of an event handler).
definitions.example: The event handler should only fire after all web page content has finished loading.

definitions.definition: To inflame; to irritate, as the passions.
definitions.example: to fire the soul with anger, pride, or revenge

definitions.definition: To animate; to give life or spirit to.
definitions.example: to fire the genius of a young man

definitions.definition: To feed or serve the fire of.
definitions.example: to fire a boiler

definitions.definition: To light up as if by fire; to illuminate.
To cauterize.
To catch fire; to be kindled.
To be irritated or inflamed with passion.
Dismissed, let go from a job.
Heated in a furnace, kiln, etc., to become permanently hardened.
A projecting part of a rampart or other fortification.
A well-fortified position; a stronghold or citadel.
A person, group, or thing, that strongly defends some principle.
To furnish with a bastion.
An old person.
Having wrinkles.
A kitten or young cat.
(sometimes capitalized) A pet name for a cat.
definitions.example: Aw, look at the kitty!

definitions.definition: A money pool, as for a card game, or for shared expenses.
In a home game, a small, specified amount taken from each pot to pay the host's expenses.
Female genitals. (Euphemistic form of pussy.)
A set of additional cards dealt face down in some games.
A male monarch; a man who heads a monarchy. If it's an absolute monarchy, then he is the supreme ruler of his nation.
definitions.example: Henry VIII was the king of England from 1509 to 1547.

definitions.definition: A powerful or majorly influential person.
definitions.example: Howard Stern styled himself as the "king of all media".

definitions.definition: Something that has a preeminent position.
definitions.example: In times of financial panic, cash is king.

definitions.definition: A component of certain games.
A king skin.
definitions.example: Oi mate, have you got kings?

definitions.definition: A male dragonfly; a drake.
A king-sized bed.
A male person.
To crown king, to make (a person) king.
To rule over as king.
To perform the duties of a king.
To assume or pretend preeminence (over); to lord it over.
To promote a piece of draughts/checkers that has traversed the board to the opposite side, that piece subsequently being permitted to move backwards as well as forwards.
To dress and perform as a drag king.
A drinking game that uses playing cards.
A sounding stone, a Chinese musical instrument.
Needing to drink.
definitions.example: After all that work I am really thirsty.

definitions.definition: Causing thirst; giving one a need to drink (informal).
Craving something.
definitions.example: After the president left office, the nation was thirsty for change.

definitions.definition: Desiring sex.
definitions.example: This is the third time that girl brought up her crush. She must really be thirsty for her.

definitions.definition: Any strepsirrhine primate of the infraorder Lemuriformes, superfamily Lemuroidea, native only to Madagascar and some surrounding islands.
Any of the genus Lemur, represented by the ring-tailed lemur (Lemur catta).
A loris (Lemur tardigradus, now Loris tardigradus), predating the 10th edition of Systema Naturæ.
A long communal housing of the Iroquois and some other American Indians, the Malays, the Indonesians, the Vikings and many other peoples.
An outhouse: an outbuilding used for urination and defecation.
A large organ in the body that stores and metabolizes nutrients, destroys toxins and produces bile. It is responsible for thousands of biochemical reactions.
definitions.example: Steve Jobs is a famous liver transplant recipient.

definitions.definition: This organ, as taken from animals used as food.
definitions.example: I'd like some goose liver pate.

definitions.definition: A dark brown colour, tinted with red and gray, like the colour of liver.
Of the colour of liver (dark brown, tinted with red and gray).
Able to be seen.
definitions.example: When the sun rises, the world becomes visible.

definitions.definition: An incident of bleeding, as in haemophilia.
A narrow edge around a page layout, to be printed but cut off afterwards (added to allow for slight misalignment, especially with pictures that should run to the edge of the finished sheet).
(sound recording) The situation where sound is picked up by a microphone from a source other than that which is intended.
The removal of air bubbles from a pipe containing other fluids.
(of a person or animal) To lose blood through an injured blood vessel.
definitions.example: If her nose bleeds, try to use ice.

definitions.definition: To let or draw blood from.
To take large amounts of money from.
To steadily lose (something vital).

definitions.definition: (of an ink or dye) To spread from the intended location and stain the surrounding cloth or paper.

definitions.definition: To remove air bubbles from a pipe containing other fluids.
To bleed on; to make bloody.
To show one's group loyalty by showing (its associated color) in one's blood.
definitions.example: He was a devoted Vikings fan: he bled purple.

definitions.definition: To lose sap, gum, or juice.
definitions.example: A tree or a vine bleeds when tapped or wounded.

definitions.definition: To issue forth, or drop, like blood from an incision.
(of a phonological rule) To destroy the environment where another phonological rule would have applied.
definitions.example: Labialization bleeds palatalization.

definitions.definition: A game (originally Chinese) for four players, using a collection of tiles divided into five or six suits.
A solitaire game using the same tiles, where the player wins by removing pairs of matching exposed tiles until none remain.
A machine or device that converts other energy forms into mechanical energy, or imparts motion.
A motor car, or automobile.

definitions.definition: A source of power for something; an inspiration; a driving force.
Any protein capable of converting chemical energy into mechanical work.
The controller or prime mover of the universe; God.
The fermenting mass of fruit that is the basis of pruno, or "prison wine".
To make a journey by motor vehicle; to drive.

definitions.definition: To move at a brisk pace.

definitions.definition: To leave.

definitions.definition: Relating to the ability to move

definitions.definition: Relating to motor cars
definitions.example: Motor insurance is expensive for youngsters.

definitions.definition: Propelled by an internal combustion engine (as opposed to a steam engine or turbine).
A legally or socially binding conceptual contract of entitlement to wealth, void of intrinsic value, payable for all debts and taxes, and regulated in supply.
A generally accepted means of exchange and measure of value.
definitions.example: Before colonial times cowry shells imported from Mauritius were used as money in Western Africa.

definitions.definition: A currency maintained by a state or other entity which can guarantee its value (such as a monetary union).

definitions.definition: Hard cash in the form of banknotes and coins, as opposed to cheques/checks, credit cards, or credit more generally.
The total value of liquid assets available for an individual or other economic unit, such as cash and bank deposits.
Wealth; a person, family or class that possesses wealth
definitions.example: He married money.

definitions.definition: An item of value between two or more parties used for the exchange of goods or services.
A person who funds an operation.
The work involved in performing an activity; exertion.
definitions.example: It took a lot of effort to find a decent-sized, fully-furnished apartment within walking distance of the office.   He made a conscious effort to not appear affected by the stories in the paper.

definitions.definition: An endeavour.
definitions.example: Although he didn't win any medals, Johnson's effort at the Olympics won over many fans.

definitions.definition: A force acting on a body in the direction of its motion.
The plant Solanum melongena.
The edible fruit of the Solanum melongena: an aubergine.
A dark purple color, like that of the skin of this fruit.
A black person (used mainly by Italian-Americans).
A 180 backside rotated invert in which the front hand is planted on the lip of the halfpipe wall
A gap in a series, making it incomplete.
An interruption, break or pause.
An unexpected break from work.
A gap in geological strata.
An opening in an organ.
definitions.example: Hiatus aorticus is an opening in the diaphragm through which aorta and thoracic duct pass.

definitions.definition: A syllable break between two vowels, without an intervening consonant. (Compare diphthong.)
definitions.example: Words like reality and naïve contain vowels in hiatus.

definitions.definition: Visible electromagnetic radiation. The human eye can typically detect radiation (light) in the wavelength range of about 400 to 750 nanometers. Nearby shorter and longer wavelength ranges, although not visible, are commonly called ultraviolet and infrared light.
definitions.example: As you can see, this spacious dining-room gets a lot of light in the mornings.

definitions.definition: A source of illumination.
definitions.example: Put that light out!

definitions.definition: Spiritual or mental illumination; enlightenment, useful information.
definitions.example: Can you throw any light on this problem?

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Facts; pieces of information; ideas, concepts.
A notable person within a specific field or discipline.
definitions.example: Picasso was one of the leading lights of the cubist movement.

definitions.definition: The manner in which the light strikes a picture; that part of a picture which represents those objects upon which the light is supposed to fall; the more illuminated part of a landscape or other scene; opposed to shade.
A point of view, or aspect from which a concept, person or thing is regarded.
definitions.example: I'm really seeing you in a different light today.

definitions.definition: A flame or something used to create fire.
definitions.example: Hey, buddy, you got a light?

definitions.definition: A firework made by filling a case with a substance which burns brilliantly with a white or coloured flame.
definitions.example: a Bengal light

definitions.definition: A window, or space for a window in architecture.
definitions.example: This facade has eight south-facing lights.

definitions.definition: The series of squares reserved for the answer to a crossword clue.
definitions.example: The average length of a light on a 15×15 grid is 7 or 8.

definitions.definition: A cross-light in a double acrostic or triple acrostic.
Open view; a visible state or condition; public observation; publicity.
The power of perception by vision.
The brightness of the eye or eyes.
A traffic light, or, by extension, an intersection controlled by one or more that will face a traveler who is receiving instructions.
definitions.example: To get to our house, turn right at the third light.

definitions.definition: (usually transitive, sometimes intransitive, stative) To have a strong affection for (someone or something).
definitions.example: I love my spouse.   I love you!

definitions.definition: To need, thrive on.
definitions.example: Mold loves moist, dark places.

definitions.definition: To be strongly inclined towards something; an emphatic form of like.
definitions.example: I love walking barefoot on wet grass;  I'd love to join the team;  I love what you've done with your hair

definitions.definition: (usually transitive, sometimes intransitive) To care deeply about, to be dedicated to (someone or something).
To derive delight from a fact or situation.
definitions.example: I love the fact that the coffee shop now offers fat-free chai latte.

definitions.definition: To have sex with (perhaps from make love).
definitions.example: I wish I could love her all night long.

definitions.definition: To praise; commend.
To praise as of value; prize; set a price on.
The action of the verb to love.
Expressing a large amount of love to other people; affectionate.
definitions.example: his loving wife

definitions.definition: A nodule of stone having a cavity lined with mineral or crystal matter on the inside wall.
A monocotyledonous plant (Allium cepa), allied to garlic, used as vegetable and spice.
The bulb of such a plant.
The genus as a whole.
A ball.
A person from Bermuda or of Bermudian descent.
A unit of time equal to sixty seconds (one-sixtieth of an hour).
definitions.example: You have twenty minutes to complete the test.

definitions.definition: A short but unspecified time period.
definitions.example: Wait a minute, I’m not ready yet!

definitions.definition: A unit of angle equal to one-sixtieth of a degree.
definitions.example: We need to be sure these maps are accurate to within one minute of arc.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural, minutes) A (usually formal) written record of a meeting or a part of a meeting.
definitions.example: Let’s look at the minutes of last week’s meeting.

definitions.definition: A unit of purchase on a telephone or other network, especially a cell phone network, roughly equivalent in gross form to sixty seconds' use of the network.
definitions.example: If you buy this phone, you’ll get 100 free minutes.

definitions.definition: A point in time; a moment.
A nautical or a geographic mile.
An old coin, a half farthing.
A very small part of anything, or anything very small; a jot; a whit.
A fixed part of a module.
A while or a long unspecified period of time
definitions.example: Oh, I ain't heard that song in a minute!

definitions.definition: Of an event, to write in a memo or the minutes of a meeting.
definitions.example: I’ll minute this evening’s meeting.

definitions.definition: To set down a short sketch or note of; to jot down; to make a minute or a brief summary of.
A prepubescent child.
A person younger than the age of majority; a minor.
A person younger than the age of full criminal responsibility, such that the person either cannot be held criminally liable or is subject to less severe forms of punishment.
A publication for young adult readers.
An actor playing a child's role.
A sexually immature animal.
A two-year-old racehorse.
Young; not fully developed.
Characteristic of youth or immaturity; childish.
A warder or jailer/gaoler; keeper of the keys in a prison.
To supply a turnkey product; to supply something fully assembled and ready to use
definitions.example: We can sell you all the parts, or we can turnkey the entire unit.

definitions.definition: Ready to use without further assembly or test; supplied in a state that is ready to turn on and operate (typically refers to an assembly that is outsourced for manufacture)
definitions.example: They wanted a turnkey solution for the entire system, but we could only provide the enclosure.

definitions.definition: A coursebook, a formal manual of instruction in a specific subject, especially one for use in schools or colleges.
Of or pertaining to textbooks or their style, especially in being dry and pedagogical; textbooky, textbooklike.
Having the typical characteristics of some class of phenomenon, so that it might be included as an example in a textbook.
To give permission, power, or the legal right to do something.
To give someone more confidence and/or strength to do something, often by enabling them to increase their control over their own life or situation.
definitions.example: John found that starting up his own business empowered him greatly in social situations.

definitions.definition: One who is empowered.
Having been given the power to make choices relevant to one's situation.
Acting with confidence.
The aggregate of past events.
definitions.example: History repeats itself if we don’t learn from its mistakes.

definitions.definition: The branch of knowledge that studies the past; the assessment of notable events.
definitions.example: He teaches history at the university.   History will not look kindly on these tyrants.   He dreams of an invention that will make history.

definitions.definition: A set of events involving an entity.
definitions.example: What is your medical history?   The family's history includes events best forgotten.

definitions.definition: A record or narrative description of past events.
definitions.example: I really enjoyed Shakespeare's tragedies more than his histories.

definitions.definition: A list of past and continuing medical conditions of an individual or family.
definitions.example: A personal medical history is required for the insurance policy.   He has a history of cancer in his family.

definitions.definition: A record of previous user events, especially of visited web pages in a browser.
definitions.example: I visited a great site yesterday but forgot the URL. Luckily, I didn't clear my history.

definitions.definition: Something that no longer exists or is no longer relevant.
definitions.example: I told him that if he doesn't get his act together, he's history.

definitions.definition: Shared experience or interaction.
definitions.example: He has had a lot of history with the police.

definitions.definition: To narrate or record.
Totally reprehensible.
definitions.example: I hope they catch the person responsible for that heinous crime.

definitions.definition: Resembling a freak.
Odd; bizarre; unusual.
definitions.example: I've heard the props and costumes in this play are quite freaky.

definitions.definition: Scary; frightening.
definitions.example: Have you met the freaky new guy who moved in next door?

definitions.definition: Sexually deviant.
definitions.example: The things she asked me to do were too freaky for me.

definitions.definition: A tool used to moor a vessel to the bottom of a sea or river to resist movement.
An iron device so shaped as to grip the bottom and hold a vessel at her berth by the chain or rope attached. (FM 55-501).
The combined anchoring gear (anchor, rode, bill/peak and fittings such as bitts, cat, and windlass.)
Representation of the nautical tool, used as a heraldic charge.

Any instrument serving a purpose like that of a ship's anchor, such as an arrangement of timber to hold a dam fast; a device to hold the end of a bridge cable etc.; or a device used in metalworking to hold the core of a mould in place.
A marked point in a document that can be the target of a hyperlink.
An anchorman or anchorwoman.
The final runner in a relay race.
A point that is touched by the draw hand or string when the bow is fully drawn and ready to shoot.
A superstore or other facility that serves as a focus to bring customers into an area.
That which gives stability or security.
A metal tie holding adjoining parts of a building together.
Carved work, somewhat resembling an anchor or arrowhead; part of the ornaments of certain mouldings. It is seen in the echinus, or egg-and-anchor (called also egg-and-dart, egg-and-tongue) ornament.
One of the anchor-shaped spicules of certain sponges.
One of the calcareous spinules of certain holothurians, as in species of Synapta.
The thirty-fifth Lenormand card.
An anchorite or anchoress.
To connect an object, especially a ship or a boat, to a fixed point.
To cast anchor; to come to anchor.
definitions.example: Our ship (or the captain) anchored in the stream.

definitions.definition: To stop; to fix or rest.
To provide emotional stability for a person in distress.
To perform as an anchorman or anchorwoman.
To be stuck; to be unable to move away from a position.
A measure of wine or spirit equal to 10 gallons; a barrel of this capacity.
Direction.
Preparation.
To prepare oneself.
To direct speech.
To aim; to direct.
To prepare or make ready.
To prepare oneself; to apply one's skill or energies (to some object); to betake.
To direct one’s remarks (to someone).
To clothe or array; to dress.
To direct, as words (to anyone or anything); to make, as a speech, petition, etc. (to any audience).
definitions.example: He addressed some portions of his remarks to his supporters, some to his opponents.

definitions.definition: To direct speech to; to make a communication to, whether spoken or written; to apply to by words, as by a speech, petition, etc., to speak to.
To direct in writing, as a letter; to superscribe, or to direct and transmit.
definitions.example: He addressed a letter.

definitions.definition: To make suit to as a lover; to court; to woo.
To consign or entrust to the care of another, as agent or factor.
definitions.example: The ship was addressed to a merchant in Baltimore.

definitions.definition: To address oneself to; to prepare oneself for; to apply oneself to; to direct one's speech or discourse to.
To direct attention towards a problem or obstacle, in an attempt to resolve it.
To refer to a location in computer memory.
To get ready to hit (the ball on the tee).
An element of decoration; that which embellishes or adorns.
A Christmas tree decoration.
A musical flourish that is unnecessary to the overall melodic or harmonic line, but serves to decorate or "ornament" that line.
(in the plural) The articles used in church services.
A characteristic that has a decorative function (typically in order to attract a mate)
To decorate.

definitions.definition: To add to.
definitions.example: The editor ornamented his plain writing, making it fancier but less clear.

definitions.definition: Someone who lives in modern times.
Pertaining to a current or recent time and style; not ancient.

definitions.definition: (history) Pertaining to the modern period (c.1800 to contemporary times), particularly in academic historiography.
A young cat, especially before sexual maturity (reached at about seven months).
A young rabbit, rat, hedgehog, squirrel, fox, beaver, badger, etc.
A moth of the genus Furcula.
A term of endearment, especially for a woman.
To give birth to kittens.
A narcotics addict, especially a heroin user.
(by extension) An enthusiast of something.

definitions.definition: Resembling or characteristic of junk; cheap, worthless, or of low quality.
Tropæum.
An object, usually in the form of a statuette, cup, or shield, awarded for success in a competition or to mark a special achievement.

definitions.definition: An object taken as a prize by a hunter or conqueror, especially one that is displayed.

definitions.definition: Any emblem of success; a status symbol.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An object taken by a serial killer or rapist as a memento of the crime.

A display of weaponry and other militaria, often captured from a defeated enemy, as an ornament designed for the purpose of triumphalist display by a victor or as a show of military prowess by a monarch.
A long mound of earth, stone, or similar material, usually built for purposes such as to hold back or store water, for protection from weather or enemies, or to support a road or railway.
To put (something) in a place where it will be harder to discover or out of sight.

To beat with a whip made from hide.
A state of concealment.
A place of concealment.
A man very concerned about his clothes and his appearance.
A yawl, or a small after-sail on a yawl.
A dandy roller.
Like a dandy, foppish.
Very good; better than expected but not as good as could be.

definitions.definition: Excellent; first-rate.
definitions.example: What a dandy little laptop you have.

definitions.definition: To consume (a liquid) through the mouth.

definitions.definition: (metonymic) To consume the liquid contained within (a bottle, glass, etc.).

definitions.definition: To consume alcoholic beverages.

definitions.definition: To take in (a liquid), in any manner; to suck up; to absorb; to imbibe.
To take in; to receive within one, through the senses; to inhale; to hear; to see.
To smoke, as tobacco.
Drunk, in the state of intoxication after having drunk an alcoholic beverage

definitions.definition: Given to habitual excessive use of alcohol.
Characterized by or resulting from drunkenness.

definitions.definition: Saturated with liquid
A characteristic or property that particular things have in common.
A structure that bars passage.
An obstacle or impediment.
A boundary or limit.
(grammar) A node (in government and binding theory) said to intervene between other nodes A and B if it is a potential governor for B, c-commands B, and does not c-command A.
A separation between two areas of the body where specialized cells allow the entry of certain substances but prevent the entry of others.
The lists in a tournament.
(in the plural) A martial exercise of the 15th and 16th centuries.

A series of sounds organized in time, employing melody, harmony, tempo etc. usually to convey a mood.

Heated argument.
Fun; amusement.
To seduce or entice with music.

To use a kayak, to travel or race in a kayak.

definitions.definition: To traverse (a body of water) by kayak.

definitions.definition: A coin-operated machine that plays recorded music; it has push-buttons to make selections.

To play (music or digital content) on a jukebox

To format or set up for playing by a jukebox.
A small child; an infant.
Anything very small.
Very small.
The act of instructing, teaching, or furnishing with information or knowledge.

An order or command.
A single operation of a processor defined by an instruction set architecture.
A set of directions provided by a manufacturer for the users of a product or service.
An abstract entity used to describe quantity.

definitions.definition: A numeral: a symbol for a non-negative integer.

definitions.definition: An element of one of several sets: natural numbers, integers, rational numbers, real numbers, complex numbers, and sometimes extensions such as hypercomplex numbers, etc.

definitions.definition: (Followed by a numeral; used attributively) Indicating the position of something in a list or sequence. Abbreviations: No or No., no or no. (in each case, sometimes written with a superscript "o", like Nº or №). The symbol "#" is also used in this manner.

definitions.definition: Quantity.

definitions.definition: A sequence of digits and letters used to register people, automobiles, and various other items.

definitions.definition: A telephone number.
(grammar) Of a word or phrase, the state of being singular, dual or plural, shown by inflection.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Poetic metres; verses, rhymes.
A performance; especially, a single song or song and dance routine within a larger show.

definitions.definition: A person.
An item of clothing, particularly a stylish one.
A marijuana cigarette, or joint; also, a quantity of marijuana bought form a dealer.
An issue of a periodical publication.

definitions.definition: A large amount, in contrast to a smaller amount; numerical preponderance.
To label (items) with numbers; to assign numbers to (items).

definitions.definition: To total or count; to amount to.

definitions.definition: To relate (a story or series of events) in speech or writing.
To give an account.
To stock again; to resupply with stocks.
The emotion of being happy; joy.
Prosperity, thriving, wellbeing.
Good luck; good fortune.

A person who does not eat animal flesh, or, in some cases, use any animal products.
An animal that eats only plants; a herbivore.
Of or relating to the type of diet eaten by vegetarians (in all senses).
Without meat.
Of a product normally made with meat, having non-meat substitutes in place of meat.
(of a person) That does not eat meat.

definitions.definition: A large number of birds, especially those gathered together for the purpose of migration.
A large number of animals, especially sheep or goats kept together.

A large number of people.
A religious congregation.

Coarse tufts of wool or cotton used in bedding.

To coat a surface with dense fibers or particles.
To issue a formal command.

Fellatio; blowjob

definitions.example: He gobbled four hot dogs in three minutes.

definitions.definition: One who blacks.
A member of the Los Angeles gang The Bloods.

A vital liquid flowing in the bodies of many types of animals that usually conveys nutrients and oxygen. In vertebrates, it is colored red by hemoglobin, is conveyed by arteries and veins, is pumped by the heart and is usually generated in bone marrow.

definitions.definition: A family relationship due to birth, such as that between siblings; contrasted with relationships due to marriage or adoption (see blood relative, blood relation, by blood).

A blood test or blood sample.
The sap or juice which flows in or from plants.
The juice of anything, especially if red.

A lively, showy man; a rake; a dandy.

Bloodshed.

To let blood (from); to bleed.

A serving of wine.

definitions.definition: A dark purplish red colour; the colour of red wine.
To entertain with wine.
To drink wine.
A boy; especially, a boy servant.
Any male servant; a menial.
A tricky, deceitful fellow; a dishonest person.
A playing card marked with the figure of a servant or soldier; a jack.
Attempted remediation of a health problem following a diagnosis, usually synonymous with treatment.
Healing power or quality.
To treat with a therapy.
To undergo a therapy.

A number, the limit of the sums computed in a process in which the domain of a function is divided into small subsets and a possibly nominal value of the function on each subset is multiplied by the measure of that subset, all these products then being summed.
definitions.example: The integral of x\mapsto x^2 on [0,1] is \frac{1}{3}.

definitions.definition: A definite integral, a limit of sums.
Antiderivative

definitions.definition: Constituting a whole together with other parts or factors; not omittable or removable
Of, pertaining to, or being an integer.
Relating to integration.
Whole; undamaged.
The act of improving; advancement or growth; a bettering
The act of making profitable use or application of anything, or the state of being profitably employed; practical application, for example of a doctrine, principle, or theory, stated in a discourse.
The state of being improved; betterment; advance
Something which is improved

definitions.definition: Increase; growth; progress; advance.

An intermediary.

The "Enter" key on a computer keyboard.
A stroke of the Enter key.

definitions.definition: To cause to go (into), or to be received (into); to put in; to insert; to cause to be admitted.

definitions.definition: A real entity, event or other fact.

An individual observer's own subjective perception of that which is real.
Loyalty; devotion.
Realty; real estate.
Someone who views a spectacle; an onlooker or spectator.
Someone who watches television.
Any optical device used to view photographic slides.
A program that displays the contents of a file.
The manager of a colliery, who directs its workings and ventilation.

To reduce (wages); to deduct from.
To cut off, bar, or destroy.

A structure attached to shore for loading and unloading vessels.
The body of water between two piers.
The place of arrival and departure of a train in a railway station.
A section of a hotel or restaurant.

definitions.definition: A device designed as a base for holding a connected portable appliance such as a laptop computer (in this case, referred to as a docking station), or a mobile telephone, for providing the necessary electrical charge for its autonomy, or as a hardware extension for additional capabilities.

An act of docking; joining two things together.
To land at a harbour.
To join two moving items.

definitions.definition: An instance of wronging someone (sometimes with possessive to indicate the wrongdoer).
The incorrect or unjust position or opinion.
The opposite of right; the concept of badness.
To treat unjustly; to injure or harm.

To slander; to impute evil to unjustly.
Incorrect or untrue.

definitions.definition: Immoral, not good, bad.

definitions.definition: Improper; unfit; unsuitable.

definitions.definition: Not working; out of order.

Egregious.
Bad (not good), inauthentic, of an inferior quality, contemptible, lacking integrity, lame, or strange.

definitions.definition: Crazy, mad, insane.
Cool, bizarre, and potentially scary.
Knowledge; the state of knowing.
To perceive the truth or factuality of; to be certain of or that.

definitions.definition: To be aware of; to be cognizant of.

definitions.definition: To experience.

definitions.definition: To be able to distinguish, to discern, particularly by contrast or comparison; to recognize the nature of.

definitions.definition: To recognize as the same (as someone or something previously encountered) after an absence or change.
To understand or have a grasp of through experience or study.

definitions.definition: To have sexual relations with. This meaning normally specified in modern English as e.g. to ’know someone in the biblical sense’ or to ‘know Biblically.’
To have knowledge; to have information, be informed.
definitions.example: He knows about 19th century politics.

definitions.definition: To be or become aware or cognizant.
definitions.example: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted (with another person).
To be able to play or perform (a song or other piece of music).

definitions.definition: A disorderly outburst, disturbance, commotion, or tumult.
To make a disorderly outburst or commotion.
A psychological disorder that causes an uncontrollable obsession with stealing without economic or material need.
Collectively, personal ornamentation such as rings, necklaces, brooches and bracelets, made of precious metals and sometimes set with gemstones.

Packed or filled tightly.

definitions.definition: Distance from the root node of a tree structure.

definitions.definition: A numeric value that quantifies a character's experience and power.

definitions.definition: A floor of a multi-storey building.

definitions.definition: An area of almost perfectly flat land.
A school grade or year.
To adjust so as to make as flat or perpendicular to the ground as possible.

To bring to a common level or plane, in respect of rank, condition, character, privilege, etc.

definitions.definition: (usually with "with") To speak honestly and openly with.
The same height at all places; parallel to a flat ground.

definitions.definition: At the same height as some reference; constructed as level with.

definitions.definition: Unvaried in frequency.

definitions.definition: Unvaried in volume.

definitions.definition: Calm.

definitions.definition: In the same position or rank.
Straightforward; direct; clear.
Well balanced; even; just; steady; impartial.

definitions.definition: Of even tone; without rising or falling inflection; monotonic.
Perpendicular to a gravitational force.

definitions.definition: Freedom provided by the cessation of activities.
Free time, time free from work or duties.
Time at one's command, free from engagement; convenient opportunity; hence, convenience; ease.
Any of the female attendants of Odin, figures said to guide fallen warriors from the battlefield to Valhalla.

definitions.definition: A person who does not eat animal flesh, or, in some cases, use any animal products.

Belief in political freedom and equality; the "spirit of democracy".
To manage, control, steer.

definitions.definition: Proceeding without deviation or interruption.
Straight; not crooked, oblique, or circuitous; leading by the short or shortest way to a point or end.

definitions.definition: Straightforward; sincere.
Immediate; express; plain; unambiguous.
In the line of descent; not collateral.
definitions.example: a descendant in the direct line

definitions.definition: In the direction of the general planetary motion, or from west to east; in the order of the signs; not retrograde; said of the motion of a celestial body.
Pertaining to, or effected immediately by, action of the people through their votes instead of through one or more representatives or delegates.
definitions.example: direct nomination; direct legislation

definitions.definition: (travel) having a single flight number.
Directly.
The interior bottom or surface of a house or building; the supporting surface of a room.
definitions.example: The room has a wooden floor.

definitions.definition: Ground (surface of the Earth, as opposed to the sky or water or underground).
The lower inside surface of a hollow space.
definitions.example: Many sunken ships rest on the ocean floor.

definitions.definition: A structure formed of beams, girders, etc, with proper covering, which divides a building horizontally into storeys/stories.
The supporting surface or platform of a structure such as a bridge.
definitions.example: Wooden planks of the old bridge's floor were nearly rotten.

definitions.definition: A storey/story of a building.
definitions.example: For years we lived on the third floor.

definitions.definition: In a parliament, the part of the house assigned to the members, as opposed to the viewing gallery.
Hence, the right to speak at a given time during a debate or other public event.
definitions.example: The mayor often gives a lobbyist the floor.

definitions.definition: That part of the bottom of a vessel on each side of the keelson which is most nearly horizontal.
The rock underlying a stratified or nearly horizontal deposit.
A horizontal, flat ore body.
The largest integer less than or equal to a given number.
definitions.example: The floor of 4.5 is 4.

definitions.definition: An event performed on a floor-like carpeted surface.
A floor-like carpeted surface for performing gymnastic movements.
A lower limit on the interest rate payable on an otherwise variable-rate loan, used by lenders to defend against falls in interest rates. Opposite of a cap.
A dance floor.
The area in which business is conducted at a convention or exhibition
To cover or furnish with a floor.
definitions.example: floor a house with pine boards

definitions.definition: To strike down or lay level with the floor; to knock down.
(driving) To accelerate rapidly.
definitions.example: As soon as our driver saw an insurgent in a car holding a detonation device, he floored the pedal and was 2,000 feet away when that car bomb exploded. We escaped certain death in the nick of time!

definitions.definition: To silence by a conclusive answer or retort.
definitions.example: Floored or crushed by him. — Coleridge

definitions.definition: To amaze or greatly surprise.
definitions.example: We were floored by his confession.

definitions.definition: To finish or make an end of.
definitions.example: I've floored my little-go work — ed Hughes

definitions.definition: To set a lower bound.
A state in which opposing forces harmonise; equilibrium.
Mental equilibrium; mental health; calmness, a state of remaining clear-headed and unperturbed.
Something of equal weight used to provide equilibrium; counterweight.
definitions.example: Blair thought he could provide a useful balance to Bush's policies.

definitions.definition: A pair of scales.
Awareness of both viewpoints or matters; neutrality; rationality; objectivity.
The overall result of conflicting forces, opinions etc.; the influence which ultimately "weighs" more than others.
definitions.example: I think the balance of opinion is that we should get out while we're ahead.

definitions.definition: Apparent harmony in art (between differing colours, sounds, etc.).
A list accounting for the debits on one side, and for the credits on the other.
The result of such a procedure; the difference between credit and debit of an account.
definitions.example: I just need to nip to a bank and check my balance.

definitions.definition: A device used to regulate the speed of a watch, clock etc.
The remainder.
definitions.example: The balance of the agreement remains in effect.

definitions.definition: Libra.
To bring (items) to an equipoise, as the scales of a balance by adjusting the weights.
To make (concepts) agree.
To hold (an object or objects) precariously; to support on a narrow base, so as to keep from falling.
definitions.example: I balanced my mug of coffee on my knee.

definitions.definition: To compare in relative force, importance, value, etc.; to estimate.
(dancing) To move toward, and then back from, reciprocally.
definitions.example: to balance partners

definitions.definition: To contract, as a sail, into a narrower compass.
definitions.example: to balance the boom mainsail

definitions.definition: To make the credits and debits of (an account) correspond.
definitions.example: This final payment, or credit, balances the account.

definitions.definition: To be in equilibrium.
To have matching credits and debits.
To weigh in a balance.
To hesitate or fluctuate.
A window or other opening that has an oval or circular shape (as of an eye).
definitions.example: One can glimpse the sky through the oculus of the Pantheon in Rome.

definitions.definition: A stone milepost (or by extension in other materials), one of a series of numbered markers placed along a road at regular intervals, typically at the side of the road or in a median.
An important event in a person's life or career, in the history of a nation, in the life of some project, etc.
To place milestones along (a road, etc.).
To plan out a project as a series of major steps.
A comic originating in Japan.
An artistic style heavily used in, and associated with, Japanese comics, and that has also been adopted by a comparatively low number of comics from other countries.
A comic in manga style, regardless of the country of origin.
definitions.example: Lately I've been reading a Brazilian manga.

definitions.definition: A lash; a pliant, flexible instrument, such as a rod (commonly of cane or rattan) or a plaited or braided rope or thong (commonly of leather) used to create a sharp "crack" sound for directing or herding animals.
definitions.example: I had to use the whip to get the sheep's attention.

definitions.definition: A blow administered with a whip.
A whipper-in.
A member of a political party who is in charge of enforcing the party's policies in votes.
definitions.example: I was going to vote against the bill, but the party whip came to see me and made it clear I needed to vote for it.

definitions.definition: (UK politics, with definite article) A document distributed weekly to MPs by party whips informing them of upcoming votes in parliament.
Whipped cream.
definitions.example: Did you want to add some whip to your coffee, ma'am?

definitions.definition: A purchase in which one block is used to gain a 2:1 mechanical advantage.
A mode of personal motorized transportation; an automobile, all makes and models including motorcycles, excluding public transportation.
definitions.example: Come on, let's take my whip so we can get there in time.

definitions.definition: A move in which one player transfers momentum to another.
A whipping motion; a thrashing about.
definitions.example: I was startled by the whip of the rope when it finally snapped.

definitions.definition: The quality of being whiplike or flexible; suppleness, as of the shaft of a golf club.
Any of various pieces that operate with a quick vibratory motion
A coach driver; a coachman.
To hit with a whip.
definitions.example: The rider whipped the horse.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To hit with any flexible object.
definitions.example: I whipped her with a newspaper.

definitions.definition: To defeat, as in a contest or game.
To mix in a rapid aerating fashion, especially food.
definitions.example: to whip eggs or cream

definitions.definition: To urge into action or obedience.
definitions.example: He whipped the department into shape.

definitions.definition: To enforce a member voting in accordance with party policy.
To bind the end of a rope with twine or other small stuff to prevent its unlaying: fraying or unravelling.
To hoist or purchase by means of a whip.
To sew lightly; specifically, to form (a fabric) into gathers by loosely overcasting the rolled edge and drawing up the thread.
definitions.example: to whip a ruffle

definitions.definition: To throw or kick an object at a high velocity.
definitions.example: He whipped the ball at me.

definitions.definition: To fish a body of water especially by making repeated casts.
To snap back and forth like a whip.
definitions.example: The pennants whipped in the wind.

definitions.definition: To move very fast.
definitions.example: The wind whipped through the valley.

definitions.definition: To move (something) very fast; often with up, out, etc.
To transfer momentum from one skater to another.
To lash with sarcasm, abuse, etc.
To thrash; to beat out, as grain, by striking.
definitions.example: to whip wheat

definitions.definition: A type, race or category; a group of entities that have common characteristics such that they may be grouped together.
definitions.example: This is a strange kind of tobacco.

definitions.definition: A makeshift or otherwise atypical specimen.
definitions.example: The opening served as a kind of window.

definitions.definition: One's inherent nature; character, natural disposition.
Family, lineage.
Manner.
Goods or services used as payment, as e.g. in barter.
Equivalent means used as response to an action.
definitions.example: I'll pay in kind for his insult.

definitions.definition: Each of the two elements of the communion service, bread and wine.
A non-SI unit of energy equal to 1,000 calories, used (now rare) in chemistry or physics; equal to 1 calorie or Calorie as used in nutrition. Symbol kcal.
